# Transcrição: 08- Report Setup & Analysis- Webinar KPIs 

- **Fonte:** 08- Report Setup & Analysis- Webinar KPIs 
- **Duração:** 09:30
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:02]

So this reporting view that I wanna share with you is using some custom metrics. So with the previous videos I've shared on creating your reporting and looking at dashboards, have been around what are standard metrics. So for Econ, for example, standard metrics are the purchase event, view content, add to cart, for example, this one is using a few custom events where I've created the event to track something customized, which doesn't come out of the box at Facebook. So these are two of them, for example, where I'm measuring people that have landed on specific pages. So my funnel for this webinar is I run an ad, they land on landing page, stand up sign up process, which says, you know, sign up and you get the webinar, an email link is sent to them, they click the link, they can view the replay actually from the email. They can also watch the live after registration. So this is like, you know, you're registering, you can watch live. And all those kind of prerecorded, but just, I just wanna show you the metrics and kind of how that works here. So these are just some of the stats from one of the webinars that I ran. Now, these are custom events. And what I want to do here is to see what it's costing me to get people to turn up to my webinar and watch live, which means that they register and watch it there, or to come back and watch the replay. And also the cost of registration. So this is people giving me their email address to watch the webinar. This is then going straight into watching live. And then this is the replay. And actually, if you look all the way down, my cost per read is overall 20 pounds across this whole webinar campaign. And my cost per getting them to watch live is 120 and watch replay is 200. But then you look at this and you see my cost for purchase is about, is 78 pounds. So 78 pounds 55. So for every purchase I'm paying that, so how does it make sense that to get them to watch live, it's costing this much. And the thing is, again, the devil is in the details. Not every campaign here, now bear in mind, I've created a custom view, which is to show me all webinar ads for this particular ad account. Not all of these ads are working on registration. So I created a separate view, which is to show me which ads are actually working on generating registrations and to get people to watch live. So now that I've narrowed it down, only five campaigns have been selected. These are the ones that are trying to get people to come into register, give me the email address and turn up live. So you can see actually my cost per read is eight pound 94 for an item of this ticket value, it's not that bad. Then my cost to get them to the live is 52 pounds.

## [03:02 - 06:05]

So you can actually work out what my conversion rate is by doing 52 divided by eight. So give or take, it's gonna be about 20%, let's say 15 to 20%. So that kind of gives you an idea of how well my conversion is from getting them to sign up to watch the live. But then there's also a number of people that come and watch the replay as well. And it's worth taking both of those into account. So I look at these metrics and I'm kind of scanning across. So looking again at my funnel, it's costing me six pounds for those people that actually went through this journey of click, watch and kind of go through, six pounds to get them into my checkout. And then overall for this particular part, 30 pounds per purchase to get them to actually buy. So let me see how many. So this was based on 96 purchases for this particular part of the campaign. Obviously it's a huge ROAS, but for webinars, for offline programs that take email addresses and you work on emails, it's not too wild to be seeing ROAS numbers like this. This is not Econ, Info Products is certainly a bit easier to get the high ROAS, but it's just to show you how I've set up a custom conversion event. So if I go to customize my column, you can see I've got cost per webinar live here. And on the left hand side, just waiting for this screen to load, I have my standard events, which are just what Facebook has set up by default. And then I've got my custom conversions. So I've got many different custom conversions set up here. And you can see various ones for different products. And the webinar ones are, they should be on this list. There they are at the bottom. So you can see they're here. So I've created many, many custom conversions, as you can see. So I can get deeper insights as to what's going on beyond the standard events. And then I can see them in my reporting so I can make more sense of them. So if we go back up to the full webinar ad stack, I can see there's 19 campaigns that were running under the full webinar. And I can scan across and then I look at my top level metrics. So really for this account, it was a fairly small campaign and we still generated a good return on ad spend. But again, I'm looking at where the good and opportunistic performances were. This one actually is quite interesting. So one pound 40 cost per reg for a PPE campaign to my warm list of prospects. So these are people that have viewed a video. And in this case, it was video views mainly. Fans and others. But for example, in this one, we were running some cold campaigns here. And this is quite important to understand for those of you that are running webinars, where you need to invest, where you make losses and where you make your profits. Now, I spent on this particular campaign,

## [06:05 - 09:06]

3,000 pounds just on video ads with no sales, no registrations, no nothing else. But I invested that in building my warm audiences. So I've got one more warm audience here. Got one more more audience here. And if I just look at my warm audiences, you can see that, looking at the stats here, I've got some pretty good stats going on. So this one, we had a really low cost per reg comparatively, but the cost per view replay and live were quite similar. But then you look at the actual cost per purchase and the row as those generated. And you can see the revenue numbers that kind of kicked in and where all that money was made. That three grand that we put in top of funnel, it kind of paid back. That's the thing about investing in your funnel when you really understand your audience and you understand the path that the audience are creating through your funnel, that you can maximize your return based on that. So like for example, if I just select this campaign here, so let's just do some analysis on what's going on and I select video engagement. I can see that for 3000 pounds, I reached two million impressions, not people. And from that, then I can see what kind of performance I got down my funnel. Only 93,000 turned into through plays, which is 15 seconds of continuous viewing at least. And my cost per view through play here was 0.03 pounds, which is probably more expensive than I'd want. I know I can get much lower than that. And then I kind of look at how people had funneled through. And from that 3000 pounds spent, 15,000 people had actually got to the end of the video. That's really quite promising because although I didn't make 15,000 sales, they are really warmed up prospects. So then when I go into my warm campaigns, I know that I'm going to get good performance from that because these are highly engaged. So that by the time I'm now reaching them and trying to convert them, then I know that I'm going to get good performance. So that's how I would use my kind of funnel building, use custom conversion events to track important measures. So one of the things that we did actually end up doing as well was to track how many people saw the offer in the webinar, how many people then clicked from webinar to cart page, for example. And the more that you track with your pixel, and we've covered that off through setting up custom events for your pixels, the more data you can collect from Facebook, the better the decisions you can make. And also you can also enrich your pixel data. So I could optimize for people that hit this pixel event, for example, see if that works and kind of play around with different optimizations. But for this particular funnel, you can see, for example, going from video views into website conversion gave us the biggest payback

## [09:06 - 09:30]

in generating, so from that 7K spend, 97 sales off the back of it. And that was quite a big campaign. I think the ticket value was about 2K. So that's a highly successful funnel and a process of understanding my funnel by looking at the right metrics, seeing how that funnel is performing all the way down and being able to make the right decisions.
